title: Varlink SeachIMage API does not have argument to configure if it should require https or not
body:
/kind feature

**Description**
Varlink SeachIMage API does not have argument to configure if it should require https or not.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Configure an insecure registry

2.  varlink call unix:/run/podman/io.podman/io.podman.SearchImage '{"name": "busybox"}'
Error: Call failed with error: io.podman.ErrorOccurred
{
  "reason": "couldn't search registry \"localhost:5000\": pinging docker registry returned: Get https://localhost:5000/v2/: http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client"
}

I would be glad if you could expose a parameter to allow searching on insecure registries, like podman cli has with the --tls-verify=false:
```
[root@localhost ~]# podman search --tls-verify=false busybox
INDEX            NAME                        DESCRIPTION   STARS   OFFICIAL   AUTOMATED
localhost:5000   localhost:5000/my-busybox
```


JFYI I need it for cockpit-podman tests: we don't have network access in the test VM, and I am setting up an local insecure registry that I want to search and pull images from.

comments.body: @KKoukiou could you try https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2305 and see if it fixes your issue?

Also I think the API.md doc file should be updated accordingly, no?
https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/API.md#func-searchimage
And if possible, add in the API docs what is the default value (true, false) for the tls-verify.

So, I found that the issue was here that I had the registry listed in insecure registries, so somehow parameter was not taken into effect.

I think we should always respect the passed args however. and have the exact same behavior as the cli.

```
**--tls-verify**

Require HTTPS and verify certificates when contacting registries (default: true). If explicitly set to true,
then TLS verification will be used. If set to false, then TLS verification will not be used. If not specified,
TLS verification will be used unless the target registry is listed as an insecure registry in registries.conf.
```
